metric_value: Many NT servers ostensibly look alike (same fundamental hardware running Windows NT Server); IS decision makers finding tremendous differences in robustness
notes: Aberdeen's framing of the NT server differentiation problem that motivates the full study

metric_value: Gathering detailed comparative information to determine best NT supplier can add weeks or months to selection process
notes: Aberdeen identifies NT supplier evaluation complexity as enterprise pain point

metric_name: Aberdeen research methodology for this profile
metric_value: Conducted in-depth interviews with IS managers who deployed NT servers; compared NCR customers against users of alternative NT platforms
notes: Aberdeen's documented methodology: comparative customer interviews

metric_value: Processing power — NCR distinguishable from Compaq HP and IBM
notes: Aberdeen's competitive positioning of NCR NT on three dimensions

metric_value: Applications focus — NCR distinguishable in transaction-intensive application support
notes: Second of three NCR differentiation dimensions identified by Aberdeen

metric_value: Service/support availability — NCR distinguishable from competitors
notes: Third of three NCR differentiation dimensions; Aberdeen's service/support assessment

metric_name: Aberdeen recommendation for NCR NT HATP
metric_value: IS decision makers looking for leading-edge HATP servers would be well-served to evaluate NCR's NT services for transaction-intensive NT applications
notes: Aberdeen's final editorial endorsement of NCR for HATP use cases

metric_value: NCR exited commodity server market post-2000; split into two independent companies in 2023: NCR Voyix (digital commerce) and NCR Atleos (ATM/banking)
notes: NCR's server business was not sustained; ATM and point-of-sale businesses became its core. The 2023 split confirmed NCR's refocus on retail/banking verticals. Source: NCR corporate announcements 2023.

metric_value: Compaq acquired by Hewlett-Packard for $25 billion in 2002; brand discontinued; ProLiant server line absorbed into HP/HPE
notes: HP-Compaq merger completed May 2002; represented consolidation Aberdeen did not foresee in 1997

metric_value: IBM sold x86/Netfinity server business to Lenovo in 2005 for $1.75 billion; IBM exited commodity server market
notes: IBM retained Power Systems and mainframes; x86 server business sold to Lenovo
